description_long: This rifle features a robust, angular receiver with a matte black finish and a prominent barrel extending forward, equipped with a series of vent holes near the muzzle for heat dissipation. The stock is minimalistic, appearing as a simple extension of the receiver, and the grip is ergonomically shaped for secure handling. Two cylindrical, red-colored magazines or canisters are mounted vertically beneath the receiver, suggesting a high-capacity or specialized ammunition system. The rifle includes a raised rear sight and a front post, both integrated into the top rail, providing basic aiming functionality. A black, tubular fore-end runs parallel to the barrel, likely serving as a handguard. The overall design is utilitarian, with exposed mechanical elements and a focus on function over aesthetics.
description: A matte black, angular rifle with a minimal stock, ergonomic grip, and two red cylindrical magazines beneath the receiver. It features vented barrel, basic iron sights, and a utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view shows the narrow barrel, front sight post, and the symmetrical alignment of the red magazines beneath the receiver.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the full length of the barrel, the vent holes, the two red magazines, and the raised rear sight.
description_view_2: Rear view highlights the minimal stock, the grip, and the alignment of the receiver and barrel.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the same features as view 1, with clear visibility of the magazines and the handguard.
